patch.value: GH-509: pm claim --next lacks candidate filters and race-loss walk — thread next filters + advance to next candidate

GH-509 (filed against npm v2026.7.10, which predates PR#502). The base atomic primitive ALREADY shipped: pm claim --next atomically recommends+claims under one lock (pm-114v, PR#502, 2026-07-10). The issue's remaining delta on main: (1) claim --next accepts NONE of pm next's candidate filters (--type/--tag/--priority/--assignee-filter/--parent/--sprint/--release) so a worker cannot scope to its lane — verified via pm claim --help on main 2026-07-11; (2) on race loss the loser fails (exit 4) instead of transparently advancing to the next ready candidate — issue proposes walk-with-bound via --max-attempts <n> / --if-available semantics (claim --next already has --if-available for the single-item case); (3) canonical single-call worker loop: while item=$(pm claim --next --author $AGENT --type task --json); do ...; done. Spelling decision (next --claim vs extending claim --next) belongs to ADR pm-pbyu grammar rules — prefer extending the existing claim --next verb, do not mint a second spelling. Interacts with pm-eqk0: any candidate-walk must ALSO skip Decision-type items or the walk funnels every lane into an unresolvable ADR.

pm claim --next accepts the full pm next candidate-filter set (type/tag/priority/assignee-filter/parent/sprint/release) and scopes ranking identically to pm next; on losing the claim race, claim --next advances to the next ready candidate under the same lock semantics, bounded by --max-attempts (default documented); exhausting candidates returns a distinct non-error empty result usable in shell while-loops; contracts/completions/docs/MCP schema updated per new-flag wiring checklist; no second command spelling introduced

author: maintainer-agent
message: Link shipped base primitive pm-114v and Decision-skip interaction pm-eqk0
